feed.title: Nkateko Tibane
feed.subtitle: Portfolio and blog of an aspiring offensive security researcher. Red team mindset. Real-world attack surface. Writeups, tools, and notes from the field.

entry.title: Mem-Test writeup
entry.summary:
Mem-Test Source code summary The program has a global variable called hint const char* hint = "//bin/sh"; Vulnerability void mem_test(char* random) { char buff[11]; memset(buff, '\0', sizeof(buff)); printf("
I know that mine is fine...see? : "); printf("%p 
", hint + 1); puts("Let's see how good your memory is...
"); printf("&gt; "); scanf("%s", buff); if(strncmp(buff, ra...

entry.title: Buffer Overflow with Global Variable Control

link.title: Buffer Overflow with Global Variable Control

entry.summary: Exploit Write-up: Buffer Overflow with Global Variable Control Challenge Overview In this challenge, we are given a C program that contains a buffer overflow vulnerability in the func() function. The goal is to exploit this vulnerability to print the contents of the correct flag file, flag2.txt, even though the binary is hardcoded to open flag1.txt. Source Code Summary static const char*...

entry.title: Binary Exploitation Docker Environment
entry.summary: Setting Up Pwn Environment Lab with Docker Reverse Engineering and binary exploitation require a well-configured environment with the right tools. Learning binary exploitation requires to learn on older environment which can be tedious to setup due to dependency conflicts, outdated packages, and compatibility issues. In this blog we’ll walk through building a docker-based pwn environment tha...

entry.title: Databases for hackers
entry.summary: Why Databases Are Important In Offensive Security Databases get often overlooked by beginners in cybersecurity. Databases are often critical targets and play a huge role in organization’s IT infrastructure and a prime target for hackers. Here’s why: Databases are high-value targets Databases hold sensitive data. If compromised, can lead to severe financial, reputational, and ope...

entry.title: Building a Comprehensive C2 Framework
entry.summary: C2 Framework Advanced persistent threat (APT) groups and offensive security professionals rely on C2 (Command and Control) frameworks to remotely manage compromised systems. A C2 framework provides the architecture, tools, and workflows required to deliver payloads, maintain control, execute tasks, and exfiltrate data — all in a stealthy and modular way. What is a C2 Framework? A C2 framew...
